text.title: rag-eval-generation-metrics Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-eval-generation-metrics enforces.

rule.statement: Compute all three RAG Triad metrics together (faithfulness, answer_relevance, context_relevance); never ship faithfulness in isolation.
rule.rationale: Together they localise where the failure is; isolation hides the cause.

rule.statement: Fix a single judge model for the entire evaluation campaign (e.g., gpt-4o or claude-sonnet-4); document its identifier in the report.
rule.rationale: Switching judge mid-campaign invalidates cross-run comparison.

rule.statement: Never compare metric values produced by different judges as if they were on the same scale.
rule.rationale: Different judges produce systematically different scores; comparison is meaningless.

rule.statement: For production monitoring sample 10-20% of queries; do not score every query online.
rule.rationale: LLM-judge cost and latency forbid online evaluation at full traffic.

rule.statement: Export per-query scores as JSONL with query_id, faithfulness, answer_relevance, context_relevance, judge_model; aggregate downstream.
rule.rationale: Append-only JSONL is the canonical format for batch eval; aggregations stay reproducible.

rule.statement: Skip when ground truth is missing for context_recall variants, or when judge LLM is unavailable.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
